trajectory.description: Observation: Starting with empty workspace on iteration 0. Analysis: Need to implement a basic spatiotemporal GNN baseline using GCN on node features (past 12 timesteps as channels) to predict future 12, with batched edge handling for efficiency; include normalization, sliding window dataset, L1 loss, and full predict.py to match eval contract exactly. This validates pipeline quickly with 5 epochs. Decision: Write model.py, train.py, predict.py from scratch, train simple GCN (hidden=32), save best checkpoint, then immediately submit_eval to get baseline mae_60min and measure iteration time.

trajectory.description: Observation: Previous run crashed with exit 139 (segfault), likely from inefficient python loop in batched edge_index construction or dataloader multiprocessing with torch_geometric. Analysis: Vectorized batching for edge_index/weight avoids loops and potential memory issues; set num_workers=0, smaller batch_size=16 for stability; simplified predict.py to let model handle batching. Decision: Update model.py with efficient forward, fix train.py/predict.py, add torch_geo test, train 5 epochs, submit eval for baseline MAE and time measurement.

trajectory.command:
pip uninstall -y torch-scatter torch-sparse torch-cluster torch-spline-conv torch-geometric 2>/dev/null || true && python -c '
import torch
import subprocess as sp
torch_ver = torch.__version__.split("+")[0]
cuda_ver = "cpu" if not torch.cuda.is_available() else "cu" + torch.version.cuda.replace(".", "")
print(f"Installing for torch {torch_ver} + {cuda_ver}")
url = f"https://data.pyg.org/whl/torch-{torch_ver}+{cuda_ver}.html"
pkgs = ["torch-scatter", "torch-sparse", "torch-cluster", "torch-spline-conv"]
for pkg in pkgs:
    sp.check_call(["pip", "install", "--no-cache-dir", "-q", pkg, "-f", url])
sp.check_call(["pip", "install", "--no-cache-dir", "-q", "torch-geometric"])
print("PyG installation completed successfully")
' && python -c '
import torch
import torch_geometric as pyg
print("Torch:", torch.__version__)
print("CUDA available:", torch.cuda.is_available())
print("PyG:", pyg.__version__)
print("Successfully imported all packages!")
' && python train.py

trajectory.description: Observation: Iteration 2 failed with segmentation fault on torch_geometric import, indicating version incompatibility between torch and the pre-installed PyG dependencies. Analysis: The pre-installed torch-geometric and related libs (scatter, sparse, etc.) don't match the torch/cuda version in the environment, causing crash. Dynamic install using the correct wheel URL from pyg.org will resolve it. The model, dataset, and predict scripts look correct for a basic batched GCN baseline. Decision: Run a command to uninstall old PyG libs and reinstall matching versions, test imports, then execute train.py (5 epochs), and submit_eval on the best checkpoint to get baseline MAE and confirm pipeline works. This will also give us time measurement for future planning.

trajectory.description: Observation: PyG and dependencies were uninstalled in prior iter (network disabled so reinstall impossible), causing total breakage. Analysis: N=207 is tiny so dense adj matmul is efficient and avoids all PyG issues; pre-normalize adj with self-loops once (standard D^{-1/2}AD^{-1/2}); update model to pure torch GCN-style layers, keep normalization/dataset/predict logic but ensure device/adj handling is clean. This gets a working spatiotemporal baseline (spatial GCN on temporal features). Decision: Rewrite model.py + minor fixes to train.py/predict.py (no PyG imports, use normalized adj), run training (5 epochs for quick baseline), then submit_eval on best.pt to obtain first mae_60min and measure full iter time.

trajectory.description: Observation: Training crashed with RuntimeError: expected scalar type Double but found Float in torch.bmm(adj_norm, x). Mean/Std printed correctly. Analysis: normalize_adj produces float64 tensor (numpy promotion in 1.0/sqrt and diag), while x is float32 from DataLoader; model/predict/dataset logic is sound, dense adj efficient for N=207, no PyG needed. Decision: Explicitly cast all to np.float32 + .float() in normalize_adj and predict.py adj calc, ensure model forward handles consistent dtypes, run 5 epochs, submit_eval on best.pt for first mae_60min baseline and time measurement.

trajectory.description: Observation: Baseline mae_60min=6.5848 (mae_avg=6.1163) with simple 3-layer GCN (hidden=64), 5 epochs; train loss decreased steadily to 0.4566, val MAE to 5.487. Analysis: Model has limited capacity for spatiotemporal patterns (no explicit temporal conv, small hidden dim); still improving so scaling hidden dim, adding layer+dropout for regularization, more epochs + LR scheduler will reduce error especially on longer horizons like 60min without overfitting (N=207 keeps it efficient). Decision: Update model to hidden=256 w/ dropout=0.1 and extra GCN layer, revise train.py for 20 epochs, ReduceLROnPlateau, explicit model args; keep predict.py; run training then submit_eval for improved score and time measurement.

Baseline from iter5: mae_60min=6.5848 with val MAE improving to ~5.49 over 5 epochs; model (GCN-style with hidden=256) and predict.py are solid, dense adj works efficiently. Analysis: Simple fix removes the invalid kwarg; 20 epochs with scheduler and current capacity should further reduce error (esp. on 60min horizon) without OOM or overfitting given small N=207. Decision: Patch only the scheduler line in train.py, keep 20 epochs + logging, run training then submit_eval on best.pt for improved mae_60min and updated time measurement.

trajectory.description: Observation: mae_60min improved to 6.4857 (from 6.5848) after 20 epochs; val MAE reached 5.416 with train loss at 0.4557, still slowly decreasing at end. Analysis: Basic GCN has limited depth/capacity and no residuals leading to potential degradation; larger hidden dim (512), more layers (6) with added skip connections will stabilize training and capture better spatiotemporal patterns; extending to 40 epochs with current scheduler allows further gains given consistent improvement and ~7min/20eps timing (fits budget). Decision: Rewrite model.py with residual GCN (hidden=512 default, 6 layers), update train.py for 40 epochs + new model init, keep predict.py unchanged; train then submit_eval for next score improvement.

trajectory.description: Observation: mae_60min improved to 3.9235 (val MAE~2.957, train loss 0.227) with residual GCN (hidden=512, 8 layers); loss/MAE still declining slowly at epoch 80, global norm used. Analysis: Per-sensor z-score normalization better captures varying sensor speed distributions (common in traffic datasets like METR-LA); adding temporal Conv1d (kernel=3) before/after spatial GCNs captures time patterns missing in pure spatial model; reduce layers to 6 + dropout=0.1 to balance compute (expect ~35-45min for 50 epochs). This ST-GCN variant should lower error esp. on 60min horizon. Decision: Update all 3 scripts with per-sensor norm + new ST model, set 50 epochs/AdamW(wd=1e-4)/patience=5, train then submit_eval.
